prompt_text:
### Demographics: 41-year-old Asian man

### Visit type: Follow-up visit (12-month follow-up)

### Reason for visit / current symptoms
Reason for visit: Routine follow-up (every 3 months) | Diagnosis context: 2 years ago Diagnosed with type 2 diabetes after ophthalmology referral for suspected retinopathy
Current symptoms: No DKA/HHS symptoms; no recent hypoglycemia episodes; no acute complaints; Intermittent GI discomfort (nausea/bloating); Nausea, reduced appetite, and fatigue over the last 1-2 months with ongoing renal function decline.

### History
- Other conditions: Chronic kidney disease Stage 4 (15-29)
ASCVD: Coronary artery disease (History of PCI/stent); Ischemic stroke or transient ischemic attack history
Body mass index category: Obese Class I
Diabetic retinopathy
Diabetic Retinopathy
Psychotic Disorder on Atypical Antipsychotics
Major Depressive Disorder
Thyroid nodule (status post lobectomy)
- Diabetes-related comorbidities: CKD: Stage 4 (15-29); HF (No); ASCVD: Coronary artery disease (History of PCI/stent); MASLD: Normal; BMI Category: Obese Class I
- Family history: Type 2 diabetes (father)
- Social history: Current smoker (10 cigarettes/day for 25 years; ~12.5 pack-years); Alcohol 3-4 drinks/week (2-3 per sitting)

### Medication information
- Current medications: Metformin 500mg twice daily; Semaglutide 1mg once weekly; Empagliflozin 10mg once daily; Olanzapine (10 mg nightly), Sertraline (50 mg daily), Rosuvastatin (10 mg daily), Lisinopril (10 mg daily), Finerenone (10 mg daily)

### Physical exam (current)
Weight 76.2 kg; Height 170.5 cm; BMI 26.2 kg/m²; Blood pressure 131/74 mmHg

### Laboratory values (with date / relative timing)
Format: YYYY-MM-DD value or relative timing (12 months ago / 9 months ago / 6 months ago / 3 months ago / current)
Order: 12 months ago -> 9 months ago -> 6 months ago -> 3 months ago -> current
- Glycemia: HbA1c (12 months ago 10.0%; 9 months ago 10.0%; 6 months ago 10.0%; 3 months ago 10.0%; current 9.9%), FPG (12 months ago 228mg/dL; 9 months ago 238mg/dL; 6 months ago 286mg/dL; 3 months ago 275mg/dL; current 283mg/dL)
- Anthropometrics / blood pressure: Weight (12 months ago 75.7kg; 9 months ago 76.1kg; 6 months ago 75.1kg; 3 months ago 74.7kg; current 76.2kg), Height (12 months ago 170.5cm; 9 months ago 170.5cm; 6 months ago 170.5cm; 3 months ago 170.5cm; current 170.5cm), BMI (12 months ago 26.0kg/m²; 9 months ago 26.0kg/m²; 6 months ago 26.0kg/m²; 3 months ago 26.0kg/m²; current 26.2kg/m²), SBP (12 months ago 127mmHg; 9 months ago 129mmHg; 6 months ago 129mmHg; 3 months ago 131mmHg; current 131mmHg), DBP (12 months ago 73mmHg; 9 months ago 74mmHg; 6 months ago 74mmHg; 3 months ago 74mmHg; current 74mmHg)
- Liver function: ALT (12 months ago 30U/L; 9 months ago 22U/L; 6 months ago 20U/L; 3 months ago 20U/L; current 26U/L), AST (12 months ago 21U/L; 9 months ago 21U/L; 6 months ago 27U/L; 3 months ago 25U/L; current 24U/L), Total Bilirubin (12 months ago 0.4mg/dL; 9 months ago 0.4mg/dL; 6 months ago 0.4mg/dL; 3 months ago 0.4mg/dL; current 0.4mg/dL)
- Kidney: eGFR (12 months ago 30mL/min/1.73m²; 9 months ago 31mL/min/1.73m²; 6 months ago 27mL/min/1.73m²; 3 months ago 28mL/min/1.73m²; current 26mL/min/1.73m²), UACR (12 months ago 133mg/g; 9 months ago 148mg/g; 6 months ago 166mg/g; 3 months ago 183mg/g; current 190mg/g)
- Lipids: LDL (12 months ago 101mg/dL; 9 months ago 94mg/dL; 6 months ago 98mg/dL; 3 months ago 99mg/dL; current 97mg/dL); HDL (12 months ago 48mg/dL; 9 months ago 48mg/dL; 6 months ago 48mg/dL; 3 months ago 45mg/dL; current 47mg/dL); Triglycerides (12 months ago 149mg/dL; 9 months ago 151mg/dL; 6 months ago 135mg/dL; 3 months ago 135mg/dL; current 143mg/dL)
- Hemoglobin: Hemoglobin (12 months ago 13.0g/dL; 9 months ago 13.8g/dL; 6 months ago 13.1g/dL; 3 months ago 13.0g/dL; current 12.4g/dL)
- Additional tests: urine ketone Not measured (Current); fasting C-peptide Not measured (Current)

### Individualized treatment decision factors
Current adherence and self-management: Moderate adherence (estimated 70-90%); SMBG 1-3 times/week; lifestyle control inconsistent.
Financial context: Patient has comprehensive insurance with minimal out-of-pocket burden and reports no financial barriers to clinically indicated therapies (budget ≤ KRW 1,000,000/month).
